title: manihot pentaphylla subsp. rigidula
intro: Manihot pentaphylla subsp. rigidula, commonly known as the wild cassava or manioc, is a perennial plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Manihot pentaphylla subsp. rigidula include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the root of the plant can be dried and powdered to make a tea, or it can be used as a juice, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
